questions.label: Which best describes your company right now?

options.label: We are still shaping the offer or product.
We have traction, but growth is inconsistent.
We are growing, but too much depends on manual effort.
We have demand and need stronger systems to scale.

questions.label: Where do you suspect the biggest constraint is?

options.label: We are not sure yet.
Marketing or lead generation.
Operations, delivery, or internal workflow.
The business system itself needs diagnosis.

questions.label: How clearly can someone understand your core value in under 10 seconds?

options.label: They usually cannot.
Some people get it, but it takes explanation.
It is clear for the right audience.
It is sharp, specific, and easy to repeat.

questions.label: How strong is your positioning in the market?

options.label: We sound similar to everyone else.
We have differences, but they are not packaged well.
We have a clear angle but need sharper messaging.
We have a strong point of view and category narrative.

questions.label: How repeatable is your path to generating qualified demand?

options.label: It is mostly random or referral-based.
We have channels, but performance is inconsistent.
We have a motion that works but needs optimization.
We have a repeatable acquisition system ready to scale.

questions.label: How connected is your customer journey from first touch to sale?

options.label: It is fragmented or unclear.
Parts exist, but the path is not cohesive.
The journey is mapped but not fully optimized.
The journey is clear, measurable, and conversion-focused.

questions.label: How scalable are your internal workflows?

options.label: Most processes live in people's heads.
Some processes are documented, but not consistently followed.
Core workflows are documented but still manual.
Workflows are documented, owned, and improving.

questions.label: How well do your tools support the business?

options.label: Tools are scattered and create more work.
Tools help, but require too much manual coordination.
Tools are mostly connected but not intelligence-driven.
Tools support a clear operating system.

questions.label: How usable is your business data for decision-making?

options.label: We do not trust or use the data.
Data exists, but it is scattered.
We have reports, but insights are not always actionable.
Data creates clear feedback loops for decisions.

questions.label: How are you currently using AI?

options.label: We are barely using it.
We use tools casually, but not as systems.
We use AI in some workflows.
We are ready to attach AI to real operating systems.

questions.label: What have you already tried to improve growth or operations?

options.label: Nothing structured yet.
Tools, tactics, or isolated experiments.
Consultants, campaigns, or project-based fixes.
We have tried to build a system and know what failed.

questions.label: What matters most over the next 90 days?

options.label: Explore what is possible.
Clarify offer, positioning, or direction.
Build a working growth or operations system.
Scale what is already working with stronger infrastructure.

questions.label: How soon do you want to move?

options.label: No clear timeline yet.
Sometime this quarter.
Within 30–45 days.
Immediately or this month.

questions.label: What level of investment are you prepared to consider if the constraint is clear?

options.label: Not ready to invest yet.
Small project or advisory budget.
Meaningful sprint or implementation budget.
Strategic implementation budget if the case is strong.

questions.label: Which solution style fits you best?

options.label: Education or resources we can use ourselves.
Strategic advisory and recommendations.
A focused diagnostic and roadmap sprint.
A partner to design and deploy the system with us.

open_response.label: What else should we know about what you are trying to scale?

hot.headline: Your Company Looks Ready for a Strategic Diagnostic
hot.summary: Your answers suggest the issue is not lack of effort. The likely opportunity is finding the system constraint and removing it with focused execution.
insights: You likely have enough traction or urgency to justify deeper diagnosis.
Your bottleneck appears connected to system design, not isolated tactics.
A 30–45 day execution roadmap could convert scattered activity into a clearer operating model.

warm.headline: You Have a System Alignment Opportunity
warm.summary: Your answers suggest there is real potential, but some foundations need clarification before heavy implementation.
insights: Your company may benefit from sharper product, positioning, or GTM alignment.
AI and automation should be attached only after the operating model is clearer.
A fit review can determine whether a diagnostic sprint is the right next move.

cold.headline: You're in the Foundation-Building Stage
cold.summary: Your answers suggest the best next step may be clarifying the basics before investing in advanced systems or AI workflows.
insights: Focus first on offer clarity, customer journey, and documented workflows.
Avoid adding automation to unclear or unstable processes.
Use the readiness guide to identify the first system layer to strengthen.
